meta.note: Americas remainder. Most South/Central American thin countries were already covered (Asturias, Menchú, Ramírez, Galíndez, Dalton, etc.). Only two new title-specific cases cleared the bar. Ecuador added as a new country. Held: El Gran Hermano (Correa defamation suit, no confirmed seizure/ban order), El gran Burundún-Burundá (soft/exile-context), the 1828 Venezuela Bentham case (author-level). Panama/Costa Rica/Honduras/El Salvador/Belize/Guyana/Suriname yielded nothing new (Noriega/Burnham 'book bans' resolve to press/import-control censorship).

entries.title: La Roca. Cementerio de hombres vivos
entries.description_ban: Prison memoir by a jailed drug trafficker accusing Correa-government officials of corruption; a Pichincha criminal-guarantees judge ordered all 10,000 copies seized from the Don Bosco printing press on 30 January 2013 for 'insulting expressions' against the president and officials.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific court-ordered seizure; documented by Infobae.

entries.title: Ten Days That Shook the World
entries.description_ban: During Belaúnde's first government the General Postal Direction retained imported books deemed 'subversive'; the bookseller Juan Mejía Baca publicly denounced the case, naming this Reed title as a work freely sold in the US yet blocked in Peru.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific postal/customs retention; documented by El Perfil (medium confidence).
